rag_rules: Generate AGREE annex blocks only in component type or implementation scopes as dictated by AADL scope rules.
Use only visible features and types from the AADL model; do not invent or infer identifiers.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees must refer only to ports or data visible at the annex scope level.
Type consistency is mandatory: bool, int, and real are the only valid scalar types in AGREE declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Use `const`, `eq`, `ass`, and `gua` with type-annotated identifiers matching AADL data types.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent paths or implementation-only entities in component type annexes.
Do not encode event ports as numeric or Boolean values unless explicitly mapped by the model.
Do not use package-qualified symbols not declared in the model or its configured dependencies.
Do not convert Boolean ports into numeric comparisons without explicit type support.
